slots.unit_lesson: Unit 3: [Lenni Lenape](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_yHvguDhJIZmrIwY3ZcW-BIhfQH5VtH2xs1KtKlnNVY/edit?tab=t.9vfaqefj1p25) - Unit 3 - Culminating Task 1: Unit 3 - Culminating Task 1

objective.content_objective: Students will complete a culminating task using the text to find evidence about the history of Lenni Lenape.
objective.student_goal: I will find answers in the text and write them (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform about the history of Lenni Lenape (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using cognate awareness and sentence frames with visual supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing written responses that demonstrate evidence-based reading and basic organization.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Before the culminating task, the teacher can review and clarify any questions that students have about the culminating task. Be sure that students know to refer to the text for the answers.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge:
1) Briefly preview 3 key vocabulary words in Portuguese with the class using simple definitions and gestures.
2) Show a short map or image and ask students in Portuguese to point out where Lenni Lenape lived, then ask them to say one word in English about the map.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Essential for discussing the past and the Lenni Lenape story.

Key for discussing cultural practices and preservation.
Connects individual and collective cultural inheritance.
Useful when identifying cultural rituals or events in texts.
Core term when naming the Lenni Lenape as a group.

tailored_instruction.original_content: The teacher can differentiate based on the tiers in a class by making a copy. Examples: Reorganize questions; Add higher order thinking questions; Translate for ELLs; Give page numbers to find answers; Scaffolding strategies based on IEPs/504s; Add on google classroom/kami for voice assist

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant inference from typical grade 3 ELL distribution; station teaching allows targeted support and differentiated tasks for reading and writing during the culminating task.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with some Levels 1-2 newcomers typical in a 3rd grade multilingual classroom.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Preview vocabulary in Portuguese and model a sentence frame with students.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Review task instructions and highlight page numbers to find answers.
phase_plan.details: Quick whole-group check of materials and roles.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model finding evidence in the text and annotate a short passage in English and Portuguese.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Explain expectations for the culminating task and circulate to support.
phase_plan.details: Use a short shared text excerpt for guided practice.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with a station of 6-8 students to scaffold sentence frames and offer Portuguese previews for newcomers; facilitate peer collaboration.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Support other stations that complete the task independently or with extension prompts.
phase_plan.details: Rotation schedule: 3 stations, 10 minutes each; bilingual teacher leads station 1 with targeted language support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick share-out of one sentence from each supported student and check comprehension in Portuguese as needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect exit notes and confirm task completion steps.
phase_plan.details: Use exit tickets to check evidence use.

implementation_notes: Prepare sentence frame strips and a small bilingual word bank for the bilingual teacher station.
Use visual timers and a rotation chart to streamline station transitions.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce the 6 cognate pairs at the bilingual station and use matching cards and quick sorting activities; refer to them as "the vocabulary list below" when prompting students. Use pairs for a quick warm-up and to build word banks for the culminating task.

Explicitly model completing the provided sentence frames with one example in English and one in Portuguese. Provide frame strips at the station and encourage students to highlight evidence phrases in the text.
Before students begin the independent task, provide a 2-3 minute Portuguese preview of the task and a 1-2 minute Portuguese review at the end to consolidate learning and check comprehension.

special_needs_support: Provide text with larger font and reduced length for students with reading IEP goals.
Allow oral responses recorded in Google Classroom/Kami for students with writing accommodations.
materials: Culminating Task printout or Google Classroom version ([Unit 3 - Culminating Task 1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1d9wxmTmMishled9o5JkHJJmI1RvoUTQhLNogWn0UY-Y/edit?usp=sharing))
Sentence frame strips (printed) and bilingual word bank
Highlighters, sticky notes, pencils, shared text excerpt

misconceptions.original_content: Students may not have a deep contextual understanding. Students may lack stamina to complete Culminating Task 1 in one sitting.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often drops subject pronouns which can lead students to produce short English sentences missing subjects (e.g., "Went to the river" instead of "They went to the river").
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full English sentences with subjects during sentence frame practice and use visual person icons (I, you, he/she, they) to cue students to include subjects.

assessment.primary_assessment: Culminating Task: Students will complete. [Unit 3 - Culminating Task 1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1d9wxmTmMishled9o5JkHJJmI1RvoUTQhLNogWn0UY-Y/edit?usp=sharing) Teachers can make their copies and differentiate as needed for class. It can be assigned on google classroom or printed out/distributed to the class.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Culminating written task based on a short informational text; teacher-scored using existing rubric.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide bilingual word bank, sentence frames with pictorial cues, allow drawing + labeling, and permit oral response recorded in Google Classroom.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames with evidence prompts, highlight key text sentences, allow one bilingual glossary reference.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Encourage full-sentence responses with evidence and one optional transitional phrase checklist; fade scaffolds.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score primarily for use of text-based evidence and basic organization; award language-credit for inclusion of complete sentences and explicit evidence phrases at appropriate level bands.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials beyond the provided task and classroom supplies; supports use only items referenced (word bank, frames, translation) and do not change core task.

homework.family_connection: With a family member, ask in Portuguese and English: "What is one thing our family remembers about our history?" Write one sentence in English and one sentence in Portuguese and bring both to class.

objective.content_objective: I am learning to answer questions to identify key details.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Part 1: Reread
Reread the text and pose text-dependent questions.
Vocabulary - PAVE
Show each of the vocabulary words and read the text from the book for each. Ask students to turn and talk about what the author might mean. Students should study the illustration and then turn and tell their neighbor what they think.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Default inference: class likely levels_3_4_dominant for Grade 2; Station Teaching prioritizes targeted small-group language work while maintaining whole-class access.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant typical for 2nd grade, with some students at lower levels requiring L1 support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead quick L1 vocabulary preview and model one Portuguese sentence per target word; show picture prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead reread and ask a guiding text-dependent question.
phase_plan.details: Picture walk, choral read, quick pairing to activate background knowledge.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support comprehension at a station with visuals and sentence frames in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read text aloud and facilitate whole-class questioning.
phase_plan.details: Teacher models finding key details and using frames; bilingual teacher circulates to scaffold.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a focused station using the 6 cognate pairs for matching and a retell mini-lesson with the Retelling Map in Portuguese support.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate partner talk and a writing station where students begin explanatory sentences.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate through 3 stations (bilingual vocab station, writing station, illustration/sequence station) using visual timers.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a 1-2 minute share-out and check for understanding in Portuguese and model quick sentence in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect exit tickets and summarize main idea.

implementation_notes: Coordinate station materials ahead: vocabulary cards, Retelling Map, and sentence frame strips.
Use visual timers and clear rotation charts to minimize transition time.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce the 6 cognate pairs at the bilingual station using matching cards and picture prompts. Teachers will explicitly point out word similarities and contrast false friends; reference these generically as "the 6 cognate pairs" during instruction.
Provide printed frame strips at each station and model completing frames in Portuguese then English. Use frames above sorted by proficiency level and encourage partner practice.

Use the Retelling Map at a station (teacher-supported) to scaffold sequence and key-detail identification. Bilingual teacher models filling the map in Portuguese and then supports students to produce English sentences.

special_needs_support: Provide sentence starters and one-on-one scribing support as needed.
Preferential seating and enlarged text/images for visual access.
materials: Vocabulary cards for the 6 cognate pairs

misconceptions.original_content:
Seeds need sunlight to germinate
All seeds are spread by the wind
Wind carries seeds to grow anywhere
Seeds can only grow in soil
Once a seed is dispersed, it can grow anywhere

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese uses prepositions differently (e.g., 'no solo' vs 'in the soil'); students may transfer L1 prepositions when describing location.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model paired sentences in Portuguese and English emphasizing prepositions with visuals (e.g., 'in the soil' = 'no solo'); provide labeled pictures and sentence frames that highlight prepositions.

assessment.primary_assessment:
Explanatory Writing
You will describe what happens above and below the soil in a garden during each of the four seasons: spring, summer, fall, and winter.
Turn and talk to a partner: What happens to the garden above and below the soil in spring? Then share your thinking with the class.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Explanatory writing paragraph using classroom Who/What chart and Retelling Map as supports.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide word banks (the 6 cognate pairs) and sentence starters; allow oral rehearsal in Portuguese before writing; permit bilingual labels on paper.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and a partially completed Retelling Map; encourage partner rehearsal using frames.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect multi-sentence responses; provide an organizer and encourage use of transitional words; reduce language scaffolds gradually.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for content (identifies above/below details for at least two seasons) and language features (use of target vocabulary, sentence-level clarity). Differentiate expectations by level band.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials added; uses only classroom charts, Retelling Map, and existing text.

homework.family_connection: Family activity: With a family member, look at an outdoor area and find one seed or plant. In Portuguese, draw it and say one sentence describing it; in English, write one sentence using a frame from class (e.g., 'The seed is ___').

Provide color-coded frame strips for each proficiency band; bilingual teacher models frames in Portuguese, then students practice in English using visuals and manipulatives.

implementation_notes: Prepare three stations with one station explicitly using Portuguese preview/review for newcomers.
Use visual timers and rotation charts for smooth transitions.

ell_support.implementation: Teacher presents the 6 cognate pairs and highlights similarities (e.g., number/número, intervalo/interval). Use quick matching cards at a station and two-minute partner checks. Refer to the 6 cognate pairs below when prompting students; do not reprint the list here.

Display the 8 sentence frames on a chart. At stations, students use the frames to label and explain placement. Provide banded frames: fill-in-the-blank strips for Levels 1-2 and full-sentence frames for Levels 3-4.
Use number line strips and counters. Bilingual teacher models in Portuguese then English, prompting students to repeat vocabulary and place counters accordingly.

special_needs_support: Provide a pre-drawn number line with highlighted tick marks for students needing visual spacing support.
Allow students with fine motor challenges to use larger counters or a movable pointer.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may believe the unit intervals on a number line correspond to a specific unit of measure. Students may have trouble with creating their own number lines.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers may use different prepositions for position (em, sobre) and may say 'em' instead of 'on' leading to confusion about placement.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model English prepositions with gestures and provide explicit bilingual mini-chants: 'on the tick mark = sobre a marca / on = em/ sobre' and practice with manipulatives.

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.4.1 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short cool-down task: place three numbers on a provided number line and write one sentence explaining placement.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide word bank with English and Portuguese cognates, allow student to point or use single-word labels.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and a partially completed number line for scaffolded placement.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Ask for a full sentence explanation using comparative language; allow use of frames as needed.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for correct placement (content) and language: Levels 1-2: label or point accurately; Levels 3-4: use a sentence frame to explain; Levels 5-6: give a clear explanation with comparative language.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses only the cool-down sheet and pencil; no additional handouts required.

homework.family_connection: At home, ask a family member to point to numbers on a drawn number line and say the Portuguese word and the English word (número / number); child practices placing counters and explaining one placement in Portuguese then English.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Default inference: typical Grade 3 class is levels_3_4 dominant; station teaching allows targeted pull-out style support and L1 preview within stations.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with a range of beginners and intermediate learners inferred.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead the Number Talk preview in Portuguese/English, teach 2 key vocabulary words and model one think-aloud.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate whole-group Number Talk, record student thinking.
phase_plan.details: Use visual place-value chart and 6 cognate cards.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a station focused on cognate awareness and guided sentence frames in Portuguese→English for students needing language support.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Run the All the Numbers station and circulate to support math reasoning.
phase_plan.details: Stations: (A) All the Numbers, (B) Mystery Number game, (C) Teacher/bilingual supported station.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead quick share-outs using target sentence frames and check select student responses for language and accuracy.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Synthesize mathematical takeaways and confirm rounding rules.
phase_plan.details: Exit ticket prompt: round a number and explain.

implementation_notes: Prepare 6 vocabulary/cognate cards and place-value visuals before class.
Use timers and clear rotation charts for stations to maintain transitions.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce the 6 cognate pairs briefly in Portuguese, then have students match English terms to Portuguese partners in the bilingual station. Use the list as a reference during station work and the closure share-out.

Provide level-banded sentence strips (3 levels) at the bilingual station. Model oral complete sentences in Portuguese then English; students practice with partners and write a one-sentence explanation on the exit ticket.
Use a large place-value chart and number line to show how rounding works. In the bilingual station, label the chart in both languages and have students point to digit positions while explaining.

special_needs_support: Provide a two-column scaffold for students who need help organizing steps (Steps / Reason).
Allow use of manipulatives (place-value blocks) and extended time for station tasks.
materials: Number lines, index cards for Mystery Number clues

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle and confuse rounding to the nearest 100 versus nearest 10.

linguistic_note.note: Phrases like 'round to the nearest' and 'depends on' may be translated literally and cause confusion for Portuguese speakers.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach the phrase 'to the nearest' in Portuguese and English and provide clear examples contrasting rounding to 10 vs 100 with visuals.

assessment.primary_assessment: [3.3.16 Cool-down](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qAkTxPBTeqvgcF2Ztn97XlxbhECE_ceS/view?usp=drive_link) I[tem UIN - VF656717](https://nj.digitalitemlibrary.com/home?subject=Math&grades=Grade%203&view=CCSS&itemUIN=VF656717)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Exit ticket: Round two numbers and write one sentence explaining each rounding choice (written short response).

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide word bank (the 6 cognate pairs) and a partially-completed sentence frame in Portuguese/English; allow oral response recorded by teacher.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames in English with a Portuguese quick-translation anchor; allow students to plan in Portuguese then write in English.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a complete English sentence explanation; offer a challenge problem to extend reasoning.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score focuses on mathematical accuracy (rounding to correct place) and presence of a reason statement; language scoring notes expected sentence-level reasons for Levels 3-4 and phrase-level reasons for Levels 1-2.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced beyond provided word bank and frames; task unchanged.

homework.family_connection: Ask a family member to give you a 2-digit or 3-digit number; practice rounding together in Portuguese and English and write one sentence about why the number rounds the way it does.

slots.unit_lesson: Unit 3: [Lenni Lenape](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_yHvguDhJIZmrIwY3ZcW-BIhfQH5VtH2xs1KtKlnNVY/edit?tab=t.9vfaqefj1p25) - Culminating Writing Task: Culminating Writing Task

objective.content_objective: Students will complete a writing culminating task explaining the importance of culture preservation.
objective.student_goal: I will write about culture preservation (writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Argue about culture preservation (ELD-LA.2-3.Argue.Writing) by using the preview-review routine and sentence frames with bilingual scaffolds appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing a short written paragraph that includes evidence and a clear reason.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Before the culminating task, the teacher can review and clarify any questions that students have about the culminating task.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge:
1) Provide a very short example in Portuguese of how families keep traditions and ask students to share one Portuguese word connected to their family.
2) Show the model response and talk through it in Portuguese for 1-2 minutes before switching to English.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Central term for the writing task about culture preservation.
Key concept students must explain and justify in their writing.
Students must cite evidence from the text to support claims.
Academic verb for referencing textual support.
Useful when referring to the source of information or perspective.
Supports discussion of practices communities maintain over time.

tailored_instruction.original_content: The teacher can provide a model response of the culminating task. Provide anchor charts for citing evidence to support students. Use a short list of the vocabulary words and their definitions for students to use. Be sure that students include their knowledge of culture preservation in their response. Students should have access to the "[Spotlight On Native Americans: Delaware Lenape](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1SF1FWvS7_XGEF66Y-zwwcdCFpPJMXWTEZqPkiA-p78Y/edit?usp=drive_link)" by Joseph Stanely, their vocabulary notebook, and [note catcher ](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OrLBdZHu8PTQa9rkrKdpcwNnPvtRW555QF0FiNffulc/edit?usp=sharing) from the previous lesson.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant typical distribution and writing task benefits from targeted small-group support; station model allows the bilingual teacher to support writing mechanics and language at one station.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with some Levels 1-2; presence of students needing sentence-level supports.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model the structure of a model paragraph in Portuguese and point out key transition words in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Display the rubric and model response and explain success criteria.
phase_plan.details: Quick review of anchor chart and vocabulary list.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate citing evidence from the model text and translating key phrases as needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Guide students through a shared writing example.
phase_plan.details: Use the model response and anchor chart for guided practice.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support a small group to plan and draft their paragraph using sentence frames and the bilingual word bank.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Confer with other students and provide writing feedback.
phase_plan.details: Rotation: bilingual teacher leads planning station; other stations include independent drafting and peer review.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Collect one exemplar sentence from supported students and provide quick feedback in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect drafts and review rubric application.
phase_plan.details: Exit slip: one evidence sentence.

implementation_notes: Provide the vocabulary notebook and the [Spotlight On Native Americans: Delaware Lenape](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1SF1FWvS7_XGEF66Y-zwwcdCFpPJMXWTEZqPkiA-p78Y/edit?usp=drive_link) model response at the bilingual station.
Prepare a small checklist for citing evidence that students can glue into their writing notebooks.

ell_support.implementation: Use the 6 cognate pairs as a quick reference at the writing station; students circle a cognate that matches their idea and use it in a sentence. Refer to them generically as "the vocabulary list below."
Provide frames for claim, reason, and evidence; model filling them out with student volunteers in English and then offer Portuguese equivalents for clarification.
Short Portuguese preview of the rubric and a 1-2 minute review to check comprehension after drafting.

special_needs_support: Allow graphic organizers for students who need writing structure support.
Permit extra time or scribe support when indicated on IEP/504.
materials: Model response ([Spotlight On Native Americans: Delaware Lenape](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1SF1FWvS7_XGEF66Y-zwwcdCFpPJMXWTEZqPkiA-p78Y/edit?usp=drive_link))
Vocabulary notebook and bilingual word bank
Anchor charts for citing evidence and rubric copy
Graphic organizers, pencils, devices for Google Classroom

misconceptions.original_content: Students may not have a deep contextual understanding. Students may struggle to understand the culminating task.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese frequently omits subject pronouns which can cause English sentences to lack explicit subjects in student writing.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full-sentence claim statements and use subject icons on the anchor chart to cue complete sentences.

assessment.primary_assessment: Culminating Task: Students will complete [Culminating Writing Task](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YWfscllds8gxVWFlfklIf3HbOUveY_lcFp8s7yAytI8/edit?usp=sharing) to demonstrate their understanding of the Lenape people and the importance of cultural preservation.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Culminating writing task scored with teacher rubric; students write a short paragraph with claim, reason, and evidence.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a short sentence frame with pictorial cues, bilingual vocabulary list, allow drawing + label as evidence.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames for claim/reason/evidence and a one-page example; allow highlighting evidence.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect multi-sentence paragraph with evidence; provide transition word checklist and encourage elaboration.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus scoring on claim clarity, connection to evidence, and basic organization; subtract minimal language form errors for lower-level writers while prioritizing evidence use.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No changes to the primary task; supports limited to allowed scaffolds (frames, word bank, preview).

homework.family_connection: Ask a family member in Portuguese to share one tradition and write one sentence in English summarizing it; bring both notes to class.

objective.content_objective: I am learning to answer questions to identify key details.
objective.student_goal: I will listen and find key details in a story (listening, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform how seeds are planted in the story (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Listening/Speaking) by using picture supports and L1 preview-review appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing an oral explanation that includes one text detail.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Book Information: A farmer and her son plant vegetables in their garden, and the wind carries a few seeds away. Birds and animals may carry some along with them on their travels. As I read your job is to listen for different ways that seeds get planted.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Before reading, teacher quickly names 2-3 verbs in Portuguese and asks students to predict actions in the story. 2) After the prediction, students say the verbs aloud in English and point to pictures; reference the 6 cognate pairs as needed.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Key verb describing farmer actions in the story.
Repeated central noun to identify planting and dispersal.
Agents described as carriers of seeds; supports Who/What chart entries.
Specific agent of dispersal mentioned in book.
Describes how seeds move in the story and supports explanatory language.
Setting for the story and comparisons of planted vs. wild gardens.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Preview the Who/What Disperses Seeds Chart in Portuguese and highlight 2 agents.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may need scaffolding…
Understanding key vocabulary…

All seeds come from flowers

linguistic_note.note: Students may overgeneralize verbs and direct translations; some Portuguese verbs have different argument structures.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model verbs in context in both languages and provide simple frames showing who does the action and what happens.

assessment.primary_assessment: Write an explanation that describes one way seeds are planted in a wild garden. Use a specific detail from the text to support your explanation. Students can share ideas with a partner before writing.

supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect clear explanatory sentences; provide prompts to include text details and reasoning.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess how well the student uses a text detail to explain one planting method and uses at least one target vocabulary word.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses only classroom charts and text; no new handouts required.

homework.family_connection: Family activity: Share the story summary in Portuguese with a family member and point to one picture. Then write one sentence in English about how a seed got planted in the story.

objective.content_objective: Students will explain whether an estimate for a word problem solution is reasonable using rounding.

assessment.primary_assessment: [3.3.17 Cool-down](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1nQYeIjFbLvMCB5IEqD5e6TG6mpCuX49p/view?usp=drive_link)

slots.unit_lesson: Unit 4 Tall Tales - Lesson 1: What is the Old West?

objective.content_objective: Students will name the time period and identify features of the Old West.

objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform about the Old West time period (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using graphic organizers and visual supports with sentence stems appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing three expanded sentences that use conjunctions because, but, and so.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Activate Prior Knowledge - Extraordinary means very unusual or far beyond the ordinary (normal). Write about an extraordinary moment in your life.

1) Present images of Old West clothing and label 3 items in Portuguese and English.
2) Ask students in Portuguese to describe one extraordinary moment from their family history, then share one English sentence using the frame.

ell_support.implementation: Use a simple cause/effect organizer labeled in Portuguese and English; students fill organizer with partner support and use it to write three sentences using conjunctions because, but, so.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may not have a sense of time period. Students may not understand the location of events.

linguistic_note.note: In Portuguese adjectives often follow nouns which can cause English adjective-noun order errors (e.g., "horse big" instead of "big horse").
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model adjective-noun order in English with visuals and sentence frames and have students practice swapping order aloud.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will apply what they learned from the read-aloud and fact sheet by writing three expanded sentences about the American West using the conjunctions because, but, and so. Each sentence must show an understanding of the historical information (e.g., pioneers, settlers, the Pony Express, the telegraph, life on the frontier). Students will choose one topic and write three sentences that explain it from different angles using conjunctions[ because, but, so](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vCcrcQFgrRkI-6MAQbCYK8PikZR7-o2l/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=113774707341024507873&rtpof=true&sd=true). After writing, students will share with a partner and explain how their sentence shows what they learned about life in the West.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short writing assignment: three expanded sentences graded with a simple rubric focused on content accuracy and use of conjunctions.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a graphic organizer with images and bilingual word bank; allow spoken rehearsal in Portuguese before writing.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence stems that scaffold conjunction use and an exemplar.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect three clear sentences with conjunctions and encourage added detail; provide a checklist for revision.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for use of content vocabulary and correct use of conjunctions; adapt language fluency expectations by level band.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced beyond the referenced fact sheet and anchor charts; supports limited to frames and bilingual previews.

homework.family_connection: Have a family member tell a short story about an extraordinary event in Portuguese; the student writes one English sentence using because, but, or so to explain the event.

objective.content_objective: Students will use number lines to compare two numbers and determine which is greater or less.
objective.student_goal: I will compare numbers on a number line (listening, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain which number is greater or less using the number line (ELD-MA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using comparative frames and cognate prompts appropriate for WIDA Levels 1-3, producing comparative statements that show reasoning.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Refer to curriculumUnit 4: Addition and Subtraction on the Number Line
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1. Demonstrate 'greater' and 'less' with concrete objects and label them in Portuguese (maior, menor), then show on the number line in English. 2. Students repeat both words and show thumbs up/down to indicate greater/less.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Key comparative term to decide order on the line
Key comparative term used for ordering

Describes where numbers sit on the line
Used as reference points for comparison
Helps determine spacing and relative size

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station teaching supports comparative practice with targeted L1 scaffolds and peer comparison tasks.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with some Level 1 newcomers

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce comparative vocabulary in Portuguese and model gestures for greater/less.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model comparison on board number line with two example numbers.
phase_plan.details: Use concrete items to show 'maior/menor' before using abstract number line.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Guide a small group with explicit comparative sentence frame practice in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate comparing two numbers and checking intervals.
phase_plan.details: Provide comparative sentence stems at each station.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support station where students compare paired numbers using manipulatives with L1 prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate station with extension challenge problems.
phase_plan.details: Rotation of three stations: concrete comparison, drawing comparison, challenge problems.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Call on students to use a frame aloud in both languages to justify which number is greater.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect 2.4.4 Cool Down and provide feedback.
phase_plan.details: Use exit ticket comparing two numbers.

implementation_notes: Provide thumbs up/down routines to quickly check comparative understanding.
Have bilingual word cards visible at comparison stations.

ell_support.implementation: Provide frames that model '___ is greater than ___' and practice with gestures; bilingual teacher rehearses frames in Portuguese first.
Use the 6 vocabulary pairs to show related words and practice quick matching before comparisons.
Students use counters and mini-lines to compare numbers; bilingual teacher guides language production with prompts.

special_needs_support: Use color-coded counters to help students visually compare positions.
Provide sentence starters written in a larger font for students with reading difficulties.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may have trouble with creating their own number lines. Students may have trouble analyzing number lines or remembering to sequence numbers from left to right.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese-English differences in comparative phrasing can cause direct translations that sound unnatural in English.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full comparative sentences in English and Portuguese and provide sentence stems for guided practice.

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.4.4 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down: compare two numbers on a number line and write a sentence explaining which is greater.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide picture cues and allow students to point or say 'maior/menor' in Portuguese or English.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and bilingual word bank to support writing one sentence.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect comparative explanation with reasons and correct interval references.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess correct comparison and one clear justification; differentiate language expectations by level.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses the 2.4.4 Cool Down only; no changes to task format.

homework.family_connection: Family game: pick two numbers and ask the child which is maior or menor using a drawn number line; child explains in Portuguese then attempts in English.

objective.content_objective: Students will represent and solve two-step word problems using equations with a letter for the unknown.
objective.student_goal: I will write and solve equations with unknowns (writing, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain and represent two-step problems with equations (ELD-MA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using translanguaging previews, sentence frames, and equation templates appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing written equations and brief explanations that demonstrate solution strategy.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Notice and Wonder: The Unknown (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Preview the phrase 'letter for the unknown' in Portuguese and English with examples; 2) Use a quick mini-lesson in Portuguese to connect unknown representation to everyday placeholders (e.g., blank boxes).

Students will compose equations to represent situations.
Action students perform to find the unknown.
Describes how situations connect to equations and diagrams.
Students choose operations to model steps in problems.
Students explain their chosen method for solving.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Activity 1: Mai’s Beads (20 minutes). Activity 2: Represent, Solve, Explain (15 minutes). co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant assumed; station teaching allows for focused translanguaging previews and practice with equation templates.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with mixed proficiency.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce translanguaging preview of equation templates and key vocabulary in Portuguese.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Guide whole-group noticing of the unknown and operations.
phase_plan.details: Use Mai’s Beads example as anchor.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a station where students translate a situation into an equation using bilingual supports.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead problem-solving station and circulate for mathematical feedback.
phase_plan.details: Stations include Mai’s Beads, Represent/Solve/Explain, and bilingual support station.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate exit share using sentence frames and check comprehension.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Confirm correctness of equations and solutions.
phase_plan.details: Collect one example from each station for quick review.

implementation_notes: Prepare equation templates with blanks and bilingual hints.
Have manipulatives (Mai’s Beads) available at stations.

ell_support.implementation: Preview key words and an example in Portuguese, then practice the same example in English; use the bilingual station for sentence production practice.
Provide fill-in-the-blank equation templates and linked sentence frames; model solving step-by-step in both languages.
Pair students so that at least one partner can support Portuguese explanations while the other records in English.

special_needs_support: Provide manipulatives and one-step checklists for students with processing needs.
Allow students to show work with pictures if written equations are a barrier.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may have difficulty with multi-step problems. Teach RDWW to determine important information and key words in the problem.

linguistic_note.note: Students may confuse the order of operations language when translating from Portuguese; phrasing such as 'do first' may be ambiguous.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model explicit step language in both languages and provide checklists that sequence operations.

supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and expect short written explanations; offer bilingual checklists.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect full written equation and explanation in English; offer extension problems.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on correct equation representation, solution accuracy, and a brief justification; language scoring notes expectation of sentence-level explanations for Levels 3-4.

slots.subject: Math

slots.unit_lesson: Unit 4 - Lesson 2: What is a Tall Tale?

objective.content_objective: Students will identify elements of a tall tale and produce sentences using new vocabulary.
objective.student_goal: I will identify tall tale elements and write sentences (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Narrate elements of a tall tale (ELD-LA.2-3.Narrate.Writing) by using sentence stems and group talk with L1 supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing four sentences that demonstrate understanding of exaggeration, character, and setting.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Vocabulary: Exaggerated, Folk Literature, Frontiersman, Bragging, Legendary, Proportions, Ingrained, Mythological Activate Prior Knowledge - What was life like in the Wild West?
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge:
1) Quickly translate 4 key vocabulary words in Portuguese and have students repeat the English.
2) In small groups, ask students in Portuguese to tell a short family story using one vocabulary word, then select one student to write an English sentence for the class.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Central to understanding tall tale style and hyperbole.
Genre label to place tall tales within cultural storytelling.
Character type commonly found in tall tales and Old West stories.
Used to describe larger-than-life figures in tall tales.
Helps students discuss exaggerated size and scale in narratives.
Connects tall tale elements to myth and legend concepts.

sentence_frames.portuguese: O personagem parece lendário porque ___.

sentence_frames.portuguese: Como as proporções exageradas moldam o significado da história?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Teachers may use an anchor chart with the sentence types. Teachers can have children turn and talk to come up with the sentence types before having to write them down. Children can work in groups of 4 where each member comes up with one sentence type and children agree and write it down. Teachers can include pictures with vocabulary words.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Team teaching for whole-class modeling and supported practice helps students internalize tall tale elements with immediate bilingual clarification.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed proficiency with need for both whole-group explicit modeling and small-group supports.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce vocabulary in Portuguese and model one example sentence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead a brief shared reading to point out tall tale elements.
phase_plan.details: Quick partner talk to recall vocabulary.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Co-model sentence types and show visuals with Portuguese labels.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Explain sentence types and show anchor chart.
phase_plan.details: Use anchor chart to demonstrate sentence construction.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support one group of four to generate sentences using stems and vocabulary.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate other groups in partner practice and writing.
phase_plan.details: Group rotations with one bilingual-supported group.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Select one student from the supported group to read a sentence and offer brief Portuguese feedback.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect writing and lead a quick reflection.
phase_plan.details: Share one strong sentence aloud.

implementation_notes: Prepare anchor chart with sentence types and visuals.
Assign groups ahead of time so the bilingual teacher knows which students need targeted support.

ell_support.implementation: Use the 6 cognate pairs as a reference during partner talk; students circle cognates they plan to use and then practice a sentence in Portuguese before writing in English.
Provide sentence stems and structure partner-turn practice; model one sentence in Portuguese then in English and have students repeat.
Display pictures with bilingual labels and an anchor chart of sentence types to support comprehension and production.

special_needs_support: Allow oral rehearsals and sentence scribing for students who need oral expression supports.
Provide a checklist for sentence types to guide students during writing.
materials: Anchor chart with sentence types and visuals
Picture cards with bilingual labels, sentence stems, writing paper

misconceptions.original_content: Students may not understand the reason for a tall tale. Some students may understand a tall tale in a literal sense.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese adjective placement differs from English which can lead to reversed adjective-noun order in student writing.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Use explicit modeling and sentence frames that show correct English adjective order and practice swapping order aloud.

assessment.primary_assessment: Today you learned about the elements that make up a tall tale. You will show your understanding by writing four different types of sentences using the new vocabulary and information from the read aloud. Choose one element of a tall tale (for example: exaggeration, a larger-than-life character, or a setting in the American frontier). Write four different types of sentences about the element. ([statement, question, exclamation, command](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RpGjEANoD3iLScQlz_oCoNQPuZ4Hsc9f/edit))

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Writing task: four sentence types about a chosen tall tale element; teacher rubric assesses content and sentence variety.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide picture-word supports, sentence stems, and allow drawing + label; permit oral rehearsal in Portuguese.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence stems and a checklist for the four sentence types; encourage use of vocabulary words.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect more detailed sentences and correct adjective-noun order; provide revision checklist.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess ability to identify element and produce four sentence types with increasing independence; account for language level when judging grammar.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No changes to the task requirements; supports limited to frames, visuals, and bilingual previews.

homework.family_connection: Families can read a short folk story in Portuguese and ask the child to write one English sentence about an exaggerated detail they notice.

slots.unit_lesson: Unit 5 Lesson 7 - 02 - Second Grade Unit Description and Summary of Key Learning

objective.content_objective: I am learning to identify strong verbs.
objective.student_goal: I will use strong verbs in my writing (writing, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain and choose strong verbs for descriptive writing (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing/Speaking) by using a Strong Verbs Anchor Chart and verb-selection activities with Portuguese supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing original sentences that replace weak verbs with stronger choices.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Strong Verbs Anchor Chart
Explain that today we will be looking for strong verbs in our book. Review the meaning of verbs with the students by showing anchor chart. Explain that as when we write descriptive words in our writing, we can also use strong verbs. Show the right side of the strong verb chart and ask students to help generate strong verbs to complete the chart.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Introduce 3 example weak verbs in Portuguese and show stronger Portuguese alternatives; 2) Ask students to translate one strong verb into English and use it in a sentence, referencing the 6 cognate pairs if helpful.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Verb example; can be replaced by a stronger verb in student writing.
Action verb from texts that students can make stronger.
Useful verb in dispersal contexts and candidates for stronger synonyms.
Action verb students may encounter in illustrations and writing.
Action verb useful for describing movements in the book.
Descriptive verb for plant growth used in student sentences.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Use of visual aids and other comprehension strategies. Simplifying Language. Word walls and labeling classroom objects. Leveraging assisted technologies.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports targeted practice of verb selection and allows bilingual teacher to model stronger verb choices.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with some students needing direct L1 examples to widen verb choices.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Show Strong Verbs Anchor Chart in Portuguese and model one example of upgrading a weak verb.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce anchor chart and explain task.
phase_plan.details: Discuss difference between weak and strong verbs with examples.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a small group to brainstorm strong verbs in Portuguese then translate to English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read text excerpts highlighting verbs and ask students to suggest stronger choices.
phase_plan.details: Use anchor chart to list candidate verbs.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate a station where students replace weak verbs on sentence strips using bilingual prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead a writing station where students write original sentences using strong verbs.
phase_plan.details: Rotate students and provide feedback on verb choices.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Invite two students to share sentences in Portuguese and then in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect final sentences and note strong verb usage.
phase_plan.details: Quick peer feedback focusing on verb strength.

implementation_notes: Prepare Strong Verbs Anchor Chart and example sentence strips.
Provide bilingual verb lists at the verb-selection station.

ell_support.implementation: Show weak vs strong verb pairs and practice in Portuguese then English; students pick stronger options for sentences using the 6 cognate pairs where applicable.
Provide frames that guide students to replace weak verbs with stronger choices and justify their choice in a short phrase.
Bilingual teacher models changing verbs in Portuguese and English and has students rehearse in pairs before independent writing.

special_needs_support: Provide word banks with pictorial cues and allow oral sentence production if writing is limited.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may need scaffolding… on when to use verbs to distinguish the meaning of verbs.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese adjective and verb placement differs from English; students may transfer sentence structure leading to awkward verb placement.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model English sentence order and provide parallel Portuguese examples; use frames that emphasize correct English ordering.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will apply their understanding of strong verbs by writing their own original sentences using 10 strong verbs identified from the book Planting a Wild Garden.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short writing task: produce several sentences replacing weak verbs with strong verbs using class anchor chart as support.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a list of strong verbs (with pictures) and frame starters; allow oral rehearsal and bilingual cueing.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide frames and a verb bank; expect multi-word answers and some sentence independence.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect multiple original sentences with varied strong verbs and justification for choices.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for verb selection strength, sentence coherence, and correct use of verb tense; adapt expectations by proficiency band.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Task uses only the Strong Verbs Anchor Chart and classroom text; no new materials introduced.

homework.family_connection: Family activity: Look at a short picture book together. In Portuguese, name one action and think of a stronger verb for it; the child writes one English sentence using that stronger verb in the Reading Log.

objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform about body parts and their functions (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using sentence frames and picture labeling appropriate for WIDA levels 1-3, producing labeled diagrams and short oral descriptions that demonstrate vocabulary knowledge.

Sentence Stems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching provides targeted practice and L1 support for vocabulary and function identification during a review session.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant typical distribution for 2nd grade emergent bilinguals

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Review body part names in Portuguese and model an English frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce review activity and expectations.
phase_plan.details: Use body outline poster for point-and-name routine.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support vocabulary recall in Portuguese and prompt students to use English frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead quick guided practice labeling body parts on chart.
phase_plan.details: Keep pace brisk for review.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a sentence-frame station focusing on using function language with bilingual prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Run labeling and movement station for kinesthetic practice.
phase_plan.details: Rotate students through three stations for review.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask one student in Portuguese to name a part and then produce an English sentence using a frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect review worksheets and summarize key vocabulary.
phase_plan.details: Quick formative check and praise.

implementation_notes: Prepare a large body outline poster with Portuguese and English labels.
Use quick kinesthetic movements to help memory (touch head, wiggle fingers, etc.).

ell_support.implementation: Provide simple frames for identification and function; model in Portuguese then guide English practice.
Highlight any cognates (e.g., stomach/estômago) and use matching cards to reinforce memory.
Pair students so a stronger bilingual peer supports vocabulary recall in Portuguese and models English frames.

special_needs_support: Use gesture cues and allow students to point to parts when explaining.
Provide extra processing time and simplified frames as needed.
materials: Large body outline poster with labels
Sentence frame strips and vocabulary cards

misconceptions.original_content: Vocabulary

linguistic_note.note: Students may confuse similar body words between Portuguese and English or misuse gendered agreement in Portuguese when planning translations.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Practice side-by-side examples and use gestures to disambiguate meaning.

assessment.primary_assessment: Chapter 3 Review worksheet

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Chapter 3 Review worksheet with short oral check-ins

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide picture-word bank in Portuguese and English and allow pointing or one-word answers.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames to complete written responses and allow brief Portuguese planning.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect short written sentences describing function with minimal scaffolding.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for correct identification of parts and one clear statement of function using target vocabulary.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No additional materials beyond worksheet and poster; preserves the original assessment format.

homework.family_connection: Play a quick naming game at home: parent points to a body part and the child names it in Portuguese and English; practice one sentence describing its function.

objective.content_objective: Students will estimate and place numbers on a number line that has no tick marks.
objective.student_goal: I will estimate number positions (listening, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform estimated positions on a number line without tick marks (ELD-MA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using estimation strategies, visual models, and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA Levels 1-3, producing estimated placements and brief explanations.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Refer to curriculumUnit 4: Addition and Subtraction on the Number Line
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1. Demonstrate estimating midpoint positions in Portuguese then English using a concrete example. 2. Ask students to say the Portuguese term for estimate (estimar) and an English sentence frame before practicing.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Key strategy when tick marks are absent
Useful for estimating positions halfway between known numbers
Reference point when estimating
Used conceptually when estimating equal spacing
Describes where an estimate is placed
Absent on this lesson, students must estimate without these physical cues

sentence_frames.english: I estimate ___ is at ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu estimo que ___ está em ___

sentence_frames.english: The midpoint is ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: O ponto médio é ___

sentence_frames.english: Put the marker near ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Coloque o marcador perto de ___

sentence_frames.english: I estimate ___ is between ___ and ___ because ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu estimo que ___ está entre ___ e ___ porque ___

sentence_frames.english: Use the midpoint to place ___ at about ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Use o ponto médio para colocar ___ em cerca de ___

sentence_frames.english: I checked spacing and placed ___ because ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu verifiquei o espaçamento e coloquei ___ porque ___

sentence_frames.english: Evidence from adjacent known numbers suggests that ___ should be placed at ___ because ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Evidências de números conhecidos adjacentes sugerem que ___ deve ser colocado em ___ porque ___

sentence_frames.english: How accurate is your estimate and how could you improve it?

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Differentiated Instruction
Small Groups
Visuals
Manipulatives
Sentence Stems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station teaching allows repeated estimation practice with bilingual scaffolds and concrete models for emergent bilinguals.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with some students at Levels 1

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce 'estimate' in Portuguese and English and model midpoint strategy.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model estimating a number without tick marks on the board.
phase_plan.details: Use a visual of a blank line and mark known anchors.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate midpoint reasoning in a small group and provide bilingual sentence frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model use of anchors to estimate and check with students.
phase_plan.details: Provide examples with concrete items.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support station where students estimate and explain using Portuguese scaffolds then translate to English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate an extension station focusing on refining estimates.
phase_plan.details: Stations: estimate, check with partner, extend with larger numbers.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead students to state one estimation and the reason in both languages.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect 2.5.5 Cool Down and give feedback.
phase_plan.details: Use quick gallery share of estimated placements.

implementation_notes: Provide anchor numbers (e.g., 0 and 10) on student strips to support midpoint estimation.
Use think-alouds in Portuguese to model reasoning for emergent bilinguals.
warnings: Students may rely on literal translations; monitor for false cognates when discussing estimation terms.

ell_support.implementation: Provide frames that scaffold midpoint reasoning and practice them in Portuguese first, then English. Use partner talk in L1 to plan the English explanation.
Provide anchor examples and model midpoint use with manipulatives; bilingual teacher models in Portuguese then English.
Use the 6 vocabulary pairs to reinforce terms like estimate/estimar and interval/intervalo during practice.

special_needs_support: Offer number lines with highlighted anchor points for students who need clearer reference points.
Allow alternative demonstration (pointing or oral explanation) for students who struggle writing.
materials: Blank number line strips with anchor numbers

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle using math language. Students may struggle estimating. Students may struggle using the number line with no tick marks.

linguistic_note.note: The English word 'actual' is a false friend for Portuguese speakers who may use it to mean 'current' rather than 'real', leading to confusion in explanations.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Avoid the word 'actual' in instruction; use 'real' or 'true' and provide clear bilingual examples when discussing accuracy.

assessment.primary_assessment:
2.5.5 Cool Down
Grade 02 Unit 4 Section A Checkpoint (English)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down: estimate and mark three numbers on blank lines; checkpoint includes similar estimation items.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow pointing, single-word labels, and use of Portuguese frames; permit drawing to show midpoint.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and anchor prompts; allow bilingual word bank.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a written sentence explaining the estimation strategy and reference to anchors.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for reasonable placement and use of estimation language: Level 1-2: correct approximate position; Level 3-4: sentence with an estimation reason; Level 5-6: clear justification referencing anchors.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses existing cool-down and checkpoint materials; no new materials added.

homework.family_connection: Family practice: draw a blank number line from 0 to 10 and ask the child to estimate where a number goes; child describes the estimate in Portuguese and then tries in English.

slots.unit_lesson: [LESSON 20: MORE PRACTICE TO REPRESENT AND SOLVE](https://newarkps.ilclassroom.com/lesson_plans/371592-lesson-20-more-practice-to-represent-and-solve?path=Wiki.11178204%2FWiki.9879318%2FWiki.10445957%2FWiki.10448414%2FWiki.19713295%2FWiki.10449449&card=8940726) [3.3.20 Teacher Guide](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SkQ3uhpthpGgKwj1_yDmvcFiSN3F8EWO/view?usp=drive_link) LESSON 20: MORE PRACTICE TO REPRESENT AND SOLVE, 3.3.20 Teacher Guide

objective.content_objective: Students will determine the information needed to solve one- and two-step word problems.
objective.student_goal: I will find needed information and solve problems (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform and Explain how to identify necessary information in one- and two-step problems (ELD-MA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using the Info Gap routine, bilingual prompts, and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing requests for information and solution steps that demonstrate comprehension.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Number Talk: Two Steps (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Model the Info Gap routine example in Portuguese with a brief role-play; 2) Have students practice asking for missing information in Portuguese and then try in English.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Central to Info Gap routine where students request missing data.
Students must request missing information in the activity.
Used in the two-step problem that involves multiplication.
Refers to the steps required to solve a two-step problem.
Students identify what the problem is asking for.
Students determine which information is the unknown to be solved.

sentence_frames.english: I need the ___ to solve this problem ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu preciso de ___ para resolver este problema ___

sentence_frames.english: Please tell me the ___ for the problem ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Por favor me diga o ___ para o problema ___

sentence_frames.english: First I ___ then I ___ to find the unknown ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Primeiro eu ___ depois eu ___ para encontrar o desconhecido ___

sentence_frames.english: The missing information is ___, so I will ___ and then ___ because ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: A informação faltante é ___, então eu vou ___ e depois ___ porque ___

sentence_frames.english: Using the Info Gap, I asked for ___ and received ___ which helped me solve by ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Usando o Info Gap, eu pedi ___ e recebi ___ que me ajudou a resolver por ___

sentence_frames.english: I solved the two-step problem by ___ and checked my work by ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu resolvi o problema de dois passos por ___ e verifiquei meu trabalho por ___

sentence_frames.english: Evidence from the Info Gap exchange shows that we had enough information to solve because ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Evidências da troca do Info Gap mostram que tínhamos informação suficiente para resolver porque ___

sentence_frames.english: What additional information would change your solution and why?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Que informação adicional mudaria sua solução e por quê?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Activity 1: Info Gap: Introduction (15 minutes). Activity 2: Info Gap: Bake Sale (20 minutes). Illustrative Mathematics Suggested Centers [Five in a Row: Mult. & Division ](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/19elX6inyALKO_YPNoiEsbzk1WoRnlO3vSGCiERWRKus/edit?usp=drive_link) [Stage 2](https://newarkps.ilclassroom.com/wikis/10194173-five-in-a-row-multiplication-3-5?path=Wiki.10465245) [Tic Tac Round ](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1GNkEWZB3kguLsuZj3W-WgfMFd4KD0hJ01QJQBiBQuqU/edit?usp=drive_link) [ Stage 1](https://newarkps.ilclassroom.com/wikis/10215279-tic-tac-round-3-5?path=Wiki.10465245) [Number Puzzles](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Tioh9f75bgn1R8R2E4-I5QkT-bdmJCV_hjWzuNndDDY/edit?usp=drive_link) [Stage 5](https://newarkps.ilclassroom.com/wikis/10007596-number-puzzles-addition-and-subtraction-1-4?path=Wiki.10465245)

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant assumed; station teaching supports Info Gap rotations with bilingual prompting and practice.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with emergent bilinguals present.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model Info Gap request language in Portuguese and demonstrate one exchange.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Explain the Info Gap routine and set expectations.
phase_plan.details: Use a short role-play to illustrate how to request information.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support one Info Gap station focusing on language for requesting information and solving a two-step problem.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Oversee problem-solving stations and check mathematical correctness.
phase_plan.details: Rotate students through Bake Sale scenario and bilingual support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead reflective discussion on what information was needed and how they asked for it.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Summarize common missing information and strategies to find it.
phase_plan.details: Collect student reflections.

implementation_notes: Prepare Info Gap cards and role-play scripts in Portuguese/English.
Have simple bilingual request sentence strips for students to use during exchanges.

ell_support.implementation: Provide bilingual request sentence strips and model the exchange; instruct students to rehearse in Portuguese then perform in English.
Use short role-play prompts where students practice requesting and supplying information using the 6 vocabulary pairs.
Give students a checklist of needed information items to mark off during the Info Gap activity; bilingual teacher models how to use it.

special_needs_support: Provide simplified Info Gap cards with fewer numeric details for students who need reduced cognitive load.
Allow role assignment adjustments so students can participate in a supported speaking role.
materials: Info Gap cards for Bake Sale scenario

misconceptions.original_content: Students may have difficulty with multi-step problems. Teach RDWW to determine important information and key words in the problem.

linguistic_note.note: Students may omit key question words when requesting information in English if they rely on Portuguese sentence structures.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full request sentences in English and Portuguese and provide sentence starters for students to use during Info Gap.

assessment.primary_assessment: [3.3.20 Cool-down](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1XLz2uLElALBr_fA6LtpAJ8pgncrl-pUN/view?usp=drive_link) [Item UIN - VF909877](https://nj.digitalitemlibrary.com/home?subject=Math&grades=Grade%203&view=CCSS&itemUIN=VF909877) [Item UIN - VF558622](https://nj.digitalitemlibrary.com/home?subject=Math&grades=Grade%203&view=CCSS&itemUIN=VF558622) [Item UIN - VF812742](https://nj.digitalitemlibrary.com/home?subject=Math&grades=Grade%203&view=CCSS&itemUIN=VF812742)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Info Gap performance: Students request missing information and solve the two-step problem; teacher rates completeness and reasoning.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to request information in Portuguese and mark needed items; provide visual prompts.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Expect students to request and record information using sentence frames and then solve with bilingual supports.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect full English requests and solutions; prompt for justification.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess completeness of requested information, accuracy of solution, and clarity of justification; language lens notes degree of independence in English expression.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Overlay uses only Info Gap materials and sentence strips referenced in the primary lesson.

homework.family_connection: Have the student interview a family member about a small real-world scenario (e.g., how many snacks needed for guests) and practice requesting missing information in Portuguese first then translate the questions to English.
